title: oxalis corniculata subsp. pilosa
intro: Oxalis corniculata subsp. pilosa, also known as the hairy wood-sorrel or hairy creeping wood sorrel, is a plant species belonging to the oxalis genus, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Oxalis corniculata subsp. pilosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Oxalis corniculata subsp. pilosa can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant's extracts in capsule or tincture form, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
